attempt_local_note: Second bounded R5.2 repair (R5.2b). Parent C1 (comparable-v3-repair-c1) is a development-baseline-accepted attempt-local child of B with no figure-agent.closed-loop-attempt-state.v1 file, so the canonical v4 repair-execution-packet machinery (which binds only from a repair_bound/repair_authorized state) cannot bind here without inventing that state. Per docs/figure-agent.md R5.2 guidance, C2 is materialized as C1's hash-bound repair child with the same minimal honest artifact set (adjudication, attribution, repaired tex, compile evidence, crops, provenance, regression) as C1.

edit.rationale: Option (a) from docs/figure-agent.md R5.2 step 6, the classic interrupted-arrow, chosen because the label sits ON the line (arrow y=4.02 == label center y=4.02). The single (1.25,4.02)--(5.95,4.02) path is split into a left plain segment (1.25->2.53) and a right arrow-headed segment (4.66->5.95), leaving a measured gap behind the label. Tail (1.25), head/arrowhead (5.95), y (4.02), color, line width, and Stealth style are all preserved; only the middle span is removed. The label node (line 75) is byte-identical. Composition preserved: the arrow still runs left-to-right across the three composition boxes with the arrowhead pointing right (increasing direction).

measured_geometry_cm.method: pdfplumber vector-line extraction for arrow endpoints + pdftotext -bbox for label word boxes. Calibration confirmed twice: (1) arrow tail panel-x 1.25 renders at pdf-x 253.796 (no arrowhead there), scale 28.3465 pt/cm (standalone applies no page scaling); (2) inverse-mapped label center pdf-x 320.41 reproduces panel-x 3.600 (== label node coordinate). panel_x = 1.25 + (pdf_x - 253.796) / 28.3465.

label_rendered_bbox.note: label bbox identical in B, C1, C2 (the label node was never edited)

clearances_after_C2.note: measured 4.409 pt and 4.136 pt gaps respectively; symmetric interrupted-arrow reading, both well above the 0.08 cm clearance floor

compile.strict_failure_cause: check_visual_clash --strict --ignore-known-fp (36 candidates: 25 text_on_path + 11 near_miss, all promotion_tier=null) returns rc 1. This is PRE-EXISTING and identical in parent C1: a read-only re-run of the same checker on C1's committed, hash-verified render (repaired.pdf 791d014e...) also returns rc 1 with the byte-identical 36-candidate set. The targeted 3 undeclared_geometry findings (UG001/UG002/UG003) that this repair addressed ARE gone (3->0). Honest correction: C1's provenance narrative called the 3 undeclared findings the 'sole cause' of strict failure, but visual_clash --strict was a co-existing strict failure in C1 as well; removing the undeclared findings alone therefore cannot flip strict to PASS. The residual visual_clash candidates are the historically-unbound schematic visual candidates (docs/figure-agent.md line 260); they are NOT chased here per the R5.2b instruction and are unrelated to the Panel B arrow repair (identical set in C1 and C2).

evidence_crops.print_scale_note: Full figure at true publication width 155.9 mm rendered at 300 dpi (1842 px wide).
evidence_crops.target_before_note: target-before-B is cropped from grandparent B's render (the arrow runs unbroken up to the label); target-after-C from C2 (a clean symmetric interrupted-arrow gap).

human_review.note: Step 10: no self-issued scaffold/review development verdict for C2. The named human (Choe Mun-yeong) was informed this Panel B defect is the next target, but the final development-baseline verdict on the C2 render stays pending; the orchestrator brings the after-render to the named human.
